title: Cobaea pringlei
intro: Cobaea pringlei, also known as Pringle's cupflower or Pringle's trumpetvine, is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and inflammatory diseases, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cobaea pringlei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Cobaea pringlei is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Cobaea pringlei is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, Convolvulaceae. It is native to the Baja California Peninsula in Mexico. This species is classified as a type of annual or perennial twining vine with trumpet-shaped flowers. The exact classification of Cobaea pringlei can be further broken down into more specific botanical categories, but it is generally considered to be a member of the genus Cobaea. Within the family Convolvulaceae, Cobaea pringlei is a distinct species that shares characteristics with other morning glories.
